principles: Use the English source PDF as the only runtime layout anchor.
Chinese target text is usually more compact, so preserve source bands and graphics before changing frames.
Do not use official Chinese reference pages during generation; use them only after the run for offline comparison.

target_language_reflow.reason: Chinese generally fits inside English source frames; expand only through ordinary region padding unless D4 records a run-local exception.

body.anti_overfit: actual point size is resolved from source_size and current-page font quantiles; this profile contains ratios, not fixed sample font sizes

body_flow.anti_overfit: actual point size is resolved from source_size and current-page font quantiles; this profile contains ratios, not fixed sample font sizes

footnote.anti_overfit: actual point size is resolved from source_size and current-page font quantiles; this profile contains ratios, not fixed sample font sizes

table_cell.anti_overfit: actual point size is resolved from source_size and current-page font quantiles; this profile contains ratios, not fixed sample font sizes

D2_translation: Prefer compact display variants for chart labels, table cells, legends, and vertical navigation.
Preserve numbers, currencies, percentages, years, and footnote markers.
D4_layout_plan: Default to preserving source page bands, chart/table geometry, and navigation anchors.
Use Chinese compact variants before shrinking below the minimum body font band.
Do not derive any rule from the official Chinese reference PDF during runtime.
D7_quality_gate: Judge Chinese visual quality by source-vs-output rhythm first and only use official Chinese after the run as offline comparison.
Fail if Chinese glyphs are too small, fragmented, or visually unlike a human annual-report layout.
